_metadata.title: QIF Neural Pathways Atlas
_metadata.description: Named neural pathways, tracts, circuits, and large-scale networks mapped to QIF hourglass bands, brain regions, DSM-5-TR conditions, and threat techniques. Organized by pathway type: neurotransmitter-specific, sensory ascending, motor descending, limbic circuits, cortical networks, and cerebellar loops. For threat modeling purposes — diagnostic category references, not diagnostic claims.
primary_sources: Kandel ER, et al. Principles of Neural Science. 6th ed. McGraw Hill, 2021
Purves D, et al. Neuroscience. 6th ed. Sinauer Associates, 2018
Stahl SM. Stahl's Essential Psychopharmacology. 5th ed. Cambridge University Press, 2021
Haines DE. Fundamental Neuroscience for Basic and Clinical Applications. 5th ed. Elsevier, 2018
notes: Pathway disruption maps are for threat modeling, not clinical diagnosis.
DSM mappings are diagnostic category references requiring clinical validation.
BCI relevance indicates which pathways are targetable by current or near-future BCI technology.
Confidence: HIGH = well-established in primary literature, MEDIUM = supported by review papers, LOW = inferred from connectivity data.

pathway_types.neurotransmitter: Pathways defined by their primary neurotransmitter system
pathway_types.sensory_ascending: Pathways carrying sensory information from periphery to cortex
pathway_types.motor_descending: Pathways carrying motor commands from cortex to periphery
pathway_types.limbic_circuit: Circuits involved in emotion, memory, and motivation
pathway_types.cerebellar_loop: Loops between cerebellum and other structures
pathway_types.association_tract: White matter tracts connecting cortical regions

pathways.name: Mesocortical Pathway
pathways.function: Executive function, working memory, attention, cognitive flexibility, motivation
pathways.bci_relevance: HIGH — PFC is primary target of non-invasive neurostimulation (tDCS, TMS). Disruption affects decision-making and impulse control.
pathways.disruption_effects: Reduced motivation, impaired working memory, flat affect, cognitive disorganization
therapeutic_applications: TMS for depression targeting left DLPFC

pathways.name: Mesolimbic Pathway
pathways.function: Reward processing, pleasure, motivation, reinforcement learning, emotional salience
pathways.bci_relevance: HIGH — DBS targets nucleus accumbens for treatment-resistant depression and OCD. Reward circuit manipulation is a primary dual-use concern.
pathways.disruption_effects: Hallucinations, delusions (hyperactivity); anhedonia, apathy (hypoactivity); addiction vulnerability

pathways.name: Nigrostriatal Pathway
pathways.function: Voluntary motor control, movement initiation, motor learning, habit formation
pathways.bci_relevance: CRITICAL — Primary target of DBS for Parkinson's disease. STN stimulation modulates this pathway. Most clinically validated BCI intervention.
pathways.disruption_effects: Tremor, rigidity, bradykinesia, postural instability, freezing of gait
therapeutic_applications: DBS of subthalamic nucleus for Parkinson's

pathways.name: Tuberoinfundibular Pathway
pathways.function: Inhibits prolactin release from anterior pituitary. Regulates reproductive and metabolic hormones.
pathways.bci_relevance: LOW — Deep structure, not directly targetable by current non-invasive BCI. Relevant for understanding side effects of DBS near hypothalamus.
pathways.disruption_effects: Hyperprolactinemia, galactorrhea, amenorrhea, sexual dysfunction

pathways.name: Dorsal Raphe → Cortex (Serotonergic)
pathways.function: Mood regulation, impulse control, emotional processing, anxiety modulation
pathways.bci_relevance: MEDIUM — TMS protocols affect serotonergic tone indirectly. Vagus nerve stimulation modulates raphe activity.
pathways.disruption_effects: Depression, anxiety, impulsivity, obsessive thinking, sleep disruption

pathways.name: Median Raphe → Limbic (Serotonergic)
pathways.function: Fear conditioning, memory consolidation under emotional arousal, anxiety regulation
pathways.bci_relevance: MEDIUM — Hippocampal-targeted stimulation affects this circuit. Fear extinction protocols modulate amygdala serotonin.
pathways.disruption_effects: Heightened fear responses, impaired fear extinction, panic attacks, traumatic memory intrusion

pathways.name: Locus Coeruleus → Cortex (Noradrenergic)
pathways.function: Arousal, alertness, attention, stress response, vigilance, fight-or-flight activation
pathways.bci_relevance: MEDIUM — VNS activates locus coeruleus. Arousal state affects all BCI signal quality.
pathways.disruption_effects: Hypervigilance, panic, inattention, disrupted sleep-wake cycles, exaggerated startle

pathways.name: Locus Coeruleus → Amygdala (Noradrenergic)
pathways.function: Emotional memory encoding under stress, fear consolidation, threat detection amplification
pathways.bci_relevance: HIGH — This pathway is why stress enhances traumatic memory formation. BCI-induced arousal could strengthen fear memories.
pathways.disruption_effects: Intrusive traumatic memories, exaggerated fear response, emotional dysregulation

pathways.name: Basal Forebrain → Cortex (Cholinergic)
pathways.function: Sustained attention, memory encoding, cortical plasticity, learning, REM sleep
pathways.bci_relevance: HIGH — Cholinergic tone affects all EEG-based BCI performance. Attention-dependent BCIs rely on this pathway.
pathways.disruption_effects: Memory impairment, attentional deficits, confusion, reduced cortical plasticity

pathways.name: Pedunculopontine Nucleus → Thalamus (Cholinergic)
pathways.function: Sleep-wake transitions, REM sleep generation, gait initiation, postural control
pathways.bci_relevance: MEDIUM — DBS of PPN being investigated for freezing of gait in Parkinson's.
pathways.disruption_effects: REM sleep disruption, falls, gait freezing, impaired consciousness transitions
therapeutic_applications: DBS of PPN for Parkinson's gait freezing

pathways.name: Dorsal Column–Medial Lemniscal Pathway
pathways.function: Fine touch, proprioception, vibration sense, two-point discrimination
pathways.bci_relevance: HIGH — Proprioceptive feedback essential for motor BCIs. Spinal cord stimulation targets this pathway.
pathways.disruption_effects: Loss of fine touch, proprioceptive ataxia, Romberg sign, impaired two-point discrimination
therapeutic_applications: Spinal cord stimulation for chronic pain

pathways.name: Spinothalamic Tract (Anterolateral System)
pathways.function: Pain, temperature, crude touch. The primary nociceptive (pain) pathway.
pathways.bci_relevance: CRITICAL — Pain is the most common target of spinal cord stimulation BCIs. Disruption has immediate quality-of-life impact.
pathways.disruption_effects: Chronic pain, allodynia, hyperalgesia, loss of pain/temperature sensation
therapeutic_applications: Spinal cord stimulation for chronic pain
DBS of PAG/PVG for intractable pain

pathways.name: Spinocerebellar Tracts
pathways.function: Unconscious proprioception — limb position and movement for real-time motor coordination
pathways.bci_relevance: MEDIUM — Cerebellar BCIs rely on this input. Disruption impairs all cerebellar-dependent motor tasks.
pathways.disruption_effects: Ataxia, dysmetria, intention tremor, impaired coordination

pathways.name: Trigeminothalamic Pathway
pathways.function: Face and head sensation — touch, pain, temperature from trigeminal nerve territory
pathways.bci_relevance: LOW — Not directly targeted by BCIs, but relevant for facial nerve interfaces.
pathways.disruption_effects: Facial numbness, trigeminal neuralgia, loss of corneal reflex
therapeutic_applications: Peripheral nerve stimulation for trigeminal neuralgia

pathways.name: Central Auditory Pathway
pathways.function: Sound processing — frequency discrimination, sound localization, speech perception
pathways.bci_relevance: HIGH — Cochlear implants are the most successful BCI. Auditory cortex BCIs for deaf individuals.
pathways.disruption_effects: Hearing loss, tinnitus, impaired sound localization, auditory processing disorder

pathways.name: Retino-Geniculo-Striate Pathway
pathways.function: Conscious vision — object recognition, color, motion, depth perception
pathways.bci_relevance: HIGH — Visual cortex prostheses (Orion, CORTIVIS) target this pathway. Retinal implants bypass early stages.
pathways.disruption_effects: Visual field deficits, cortical blindness, visual agnosia, prosopagnosia

pathways.name: Vestibular Pathway
pathways.function: Balance, spatial orientation, vestibulo-ocular reflex, postural control
pathways.bci_relevance: MEDIUM — Vestibular implants under development. GVS (galvanic vestibular stimulation) is a known attack vector.
pathways.disruption_effects: Vertigo, nystagmus, balance loss, spatial disorientation, motion sickness

pathways.name: Olfactory Pathway
pathways.function: Smell processing — only sensory modality that bypasses thalamic relay. Direct limbic access.
pathways.bci_relevance: LOW — No current BCIs target olfaction. But direct limbic access makes it a theoretical attack vector for emotional manipulation.
pathways.disruption_effects: Anosmia, parosmia, impaired taste, reduced emotional associations with smell

pathways.name: Corticospinal Tract (Pyramidal)
pathways.function: Voluntary movement — fine motor control of limbs, especially distal muscles (hands, fingers)
pathways.bci_relevance: CRITICAL — Motor BCIs decode signals from this tract. BrainGate, Neuralink target motor cortex neurons feeding this pathway.
pathways.disruption_effects: Spastic paralysis, hyperreflexia, Babinski sign, loss of fine motor control

pathways.name: Corticobulbar Tract
pathways.function: Motor control of face, jaw, tongue, pharynx, larynx — speech and swallowing
pathways.bci_relevance: HIGH — Speech BCIs decode signals from cortical regions feeding this tract.
pathways.disruption_effects: Dysarthria, dysphagia, facial droop, pseudobulbar palsy, emotional lability

pathways.name: Rubrospinal Tract
pathways.function: Flexor motor control — assists corticospinal tract for upper limb movements
pathways.bci_relevance: LOW — Not directly targeted by BCIs. May serve as compensatory pathway after corticospinal damage.
pathways.disruption_effects: Reduced flexor tone in upper limbs (subtle, masked by corticospinal function)

pathways.name: Vestibulospinal Tract
pathways.function: Postural control — maintains balance by activating extensor muscles in response to vestibular input
pathways.bci_relevance: MEDIUM — GVS (galvanic vestibular stimulation) modulates this pathway. Used in balance rehabilitation.
pathways.disruption_effects: Postural instability, falls, impaired balance responses

pathways.name: Reticulospinal Tract
pathways.function: Automatic posture, locomotion, muscle tone regulation, respiratory motor neurons
pathways.bci_relevance: MEDIUM — Spinal cord stimulation for locomotion recovery targets neurons receiving reticulospinal input.
pathways.disruption_effects: Abnormal muscle tone, impaired automatic postural adjustments, locomotion deficits
therapeutic_applications: Epidural stimulation for locomotion after SCI

pathways.name: Tectospinal Tract
pathways.function: Head and neck orientation toward visual and auditory stimuli — reflexive gaze stabilization
pathways.bci_relevance: LOW — Not directly targeted. Relevant for understanding gaze-tracking BCI interference.
pathways.disruption_effects: Impaired reflexive head turning toward stimuli

pathways.name: Corticopontocerebellar Pathway
pathways.function: Sends motor plan copy to cerebellum for error correction and timing adjustment
pathways.bci_relevance: MEDIUM — Cerebellar processing of motor commands is critical for smooth BCI-controlled movement.
pathways.disruption_effects: Dysmetria, intention tremor, decomposition of movement
therapeutic_applications: Cerebellar tDCS for motor learning enhancement

pathways.name: Papez Circuit
pathways.function: Episodic memory formation and emotional context. The core memory circuit — hippocampus → fornix → mammillary bodies → anterior thalamus → cingulate → hippocampus.
pathways.bci_relevance: HIGH — Memory prosthetics (DARPA RAM) target hippocampal nodes of this circuit. Memory manipulation is a dual-use concern.
pathways.disruption_effects: Anterograde amnesia, confabulation, impaired emotional memory, Korsakoff syndrome

pathways.name: Amygdala–Prefrontal Circuit
pathways.function: Top-down emotion regulation — PFC inhibits amygdala fear response. Bidirectional: amygdala signals threat to PFC for appraisal.
pathways.bci_relevance: HIGH — TMS to DLPFC modulates this circuit for depression and anxiety. Neurofeedback targets amygdala-PFC connectivity.
pathways.disruption_effects: Anxiety, impaired fear extinction, emotional dysregulation, impulsive aggression

pathways.name: Hippocampal–Prefrontal Circuit
pathways.function: Memory consolidation, contextual memory retrieval, working memory maintenance, spatial decision-making
pathways.bci_relevance: HIGH — Memory BCIs must interface with this circuit. Sleep replay (sharp-wave ripples) consolidates through this pathway.
pathways.disruption_effects: Memory consolidation failure, contextual confusion, disorganized thinking, spatial disorientation

pathways.name: Cortico-Striato-Thalamo-Cortical Loop
pathways.function: Action selection, habit formation, reward-based decision making, impulse control. Multiple parallel loops (motor, cognitive, limbic, oculomotor).
pathways.bci_relevance: CRITICAL — DBS targets nodes of this loop (STN, GPi) for multiple conditions. Most common DBS target system.
pathways.disruption_effects: Motor: bradykinesia, rigidity. Cognitive: perseveration, impaired set-shifting. Limbic: OCD, compulsions, tics
therapeutic_applications: DBS of centromedian thalamus for Tourette's

pathways.name: Reward Circuit (Extended)
pathways.function: Reward prediction, motivation, pleasure, reinforcement learning. Integrates VTA dopamine with cortical evaluation and limbic salience.
pathways.bci_relevance: CRITICAL — Highest dual-use concern. DBS of nucleus accumbens treats depression but could theoretically induce compulsive states. Reward manipulation is the clearest BCI abuse vector.
pathways.disruption_effects: Addiction, compulsive behavior, anhedonia, apathy, inability to experience pleasure
therapeutic_applications: DBS of nucleus accumbens for treatment-resistant depression

pathways.name: Fear/Threat Circuit
pathways.function: Threat detection, fear conditioning, autonomic fear response (heart rate, cortisol), fear extinction via PFC inhibition
pathways.bci_relevance: HIGH — Fear circuit manipulation is a primary TARA concern. Both attack vector (inducing fear) and therapeutic target (fear extinction for PTSD).
pathways.disruption_effects: Pathological fear, panic attacks, hypervigilance, inability to extinguish learned fears

pathways.name: Default Mode Network (DMN)
pathways.function: Self-referential thinking, mind-wandering, autobiographical memory, theory of mind, internal mentation. Active at rest, suppressed during tasks.
pathways.bci_relevance: HIGH — DMN activity interferes with task-based BCIs. Neurofeedback can train DMN suppression. Aberrant DMN activity is a biomarker for multiple disorders.
pathways.disruption_effects: Rumination (hyperactive DMN), thought disorganization, impaired self-awareness, social cognition deficits

pathways.name: Salience Network (SN)
pathways.function: Detects salient stimuli and switches between DMN (internal) and CEN (external) processing. The brain's attention router.
pathways.bci_relevance: HIGH — Salience network dysfunction disrupts all attention-dependent BCIs. Insula is emerging as a key BCI target for interoception.
pathways.disruption_effects: Aberrant salience (psychosis), hypervigilance (anxiety), failure to switch attention modes

pathways.name: Central Executive Network (CEN)
pathways.function: Goal-directed attention, working memory manipulation, cognitive control, problem-solving, task switching
pathways.bci_relevance: HIGH — Task-based BCIs rely on CEN engagement. P300-based BCIs measure CEN output.
pathways.disruption_effects: Distractibility, working memory deficits, poor cognitive control, inability to suppress irrelevant information
therapeutic_applications: tDCS over DLPFC for cognitive enhancement

pathways.name: Language Network (Arcuate Fasciculus)
pathways.function: Language production and comprehension — connects Broca's area (speech production) with Wernicke's area (speech comprehension). Bidirectional.
pathways.bci_relevance: HIGH — Speech BCIs decode from areas connected by this tract. Disruption causes conduction aphasia.
pathways.disruption_effects: Conduction aphasia (can understand and produce speech but cannot repeat), paraphasia

pathways.name: Uncinate Fasciculus
pathways.function: Connects ventromedial PFC to anterior temporal lobe and amygdala. Emotional evaluation of memories, social cognition, naming.
pathways.bci_relevance: MEDIUM — White matter integrity of this tract is a biomarker for psychopathy, anxiety, and depression.
pathways.disruption_effects: Impaired emotional decision-making, anomia, social cognition deficits

pathways.name: Cingulum Bundle
pathways.function: Connects cingulate cortex to hippocampus and medial PFC. Emotional regulation, pain processing, memory retrieval, attention.
pathways.bci_relevance: MEDIUM — Cingulotomy (severing this tract) was historically used for intractable pain and OCD. DBS of cingulate targets this system.
pathways.disruption_effects: Emotional blunting, impaired pain modulation, memory retrieval deficits
therapeutic_applications: DBS of subgenual cingulate for depression

pathways.name: Fornix
pathways.function: Primary output pathway of hippocampus. Carries memory signals to mammillary bodies and septal nuclei. Essential for episodic memory.
pathways.bci_relevance: HIGH — DBS of fornix investigated for Alzheimer's disease (phase II trials). Damage causes severe amnesia.
pathways.disruption_effects: Severe anterograde amnesia, Korsakoff-like syndrome
therapeutic_applications: DBS of fornix for Alzheimer's (ADvance trial)

pathways.name: Corpus Callosum
pathways.function: Largest white matter tract. Connects left and right hemispheres for interhemispheric integration of motor, sensory, and cognitive information.
pathways.bci_relevance: MEDIUM — Callosotomy for epilepsy creates split-brain. Bilateral BCI designs rely on callosal integration.
pathways.disruption_effects: Split-brain syndrome, alien hand syndrome, impaired bimanual coordination, disconnection syndromes

pathways.name: Cerebello-Thalamo-Cortical Loop
pathways.function: Cerebellar output to cortex via thalamus. Error correction for ongoing movements, timing, motor learning, and cognitive functions.
pathways.bci_relevance: HIGH — DBS of VIM thalamus for essential tremor interrupts this loop. Cerebellar contribution to motor BCI accuracy.
pathways.disruption_effects: Tremor, dysmetria, dysdiadochokinesia, impaired motor timing and learning
therapeutic_applications: DBS of VIM for essential tremor

pathways.name: Cerebello-Vestibular Circuit
pathways.function: Balance calibration — cerebellum adjusts vestibular reflexes based on learned patterns. Adapts vestibulo-ocular reflex.
pathways.bci_relevance: MEDIUM — Vestibular prosthetics interface with this circuit. GVS affects cerebellar-vestibular processing.
pathways.disruption_effects: Truncal ataxia, gait instability, vertigo, nystagmus

pathways.name: Thalamocortical Relay
pathways.function: The brain's central relay — gates ALL sensory input (except olfaction) to cortex. Thalamic reticular nucleus acts as attentional filter.
pathways.bci_relevance: CRITICAL — Thalamus is the brain's firewall in QIF architecture. Disruption of thalamic gating affects all cortical processing. DBS of centromedian thalamus for consciousness disorders.
pathways.disruption_effects: Absence seizures, thalamic pain syndrome, sensory gating failure, consciousness disorders, coma
therapeutic_applications: DBS of anterior thalamus for epilepsy (SANTE trial)
DBS of centromedian for consciousness restoration

pathways.name: Hypothalamic-Autonomic Pathway
pathways.function: Autonomic regulation — body temperature, hunger, thirst, circadian rhythm, blood pressure, heart rate, fight-or-flight vs rest-and-digest
pathways.bci_relevance: HIGH — Hypothalamic DBS for cluster headaches and obesity. Autonomic disruption is a critical safety concern for any deep brain BCI.
pathways.disruption_effects: Autonomic dysregulation, temperature instability, appetite disorders, sleep-wake disruption, blood pressure crises
therapeutic_applications: DBS of posterior hypothalamus for cluster headaches

pathways.name: Vagal (Parasympathetic) Pathway
pathways.function: Bidirectional gut-brain axis via vagus nerve. Controls heart rate, digestion, inflammation, and sends visceral sensory information to brain.
pathways.bci_relevance: CRITICAL — VNS (vagus nerve stimulation) is FDA-approved for epilepsy and depression. Most widely deployed neural stimulation BCI after cochlear implants.
pathways.disruption_effects: Cardiac arrhythmia, gastroparesis, inflammation dysregulation, impaired interoception

pathways.name: Sympathetic Outflow (Thoracolumbar)
pathways.function: Fight-or-flight: increases heart rate, dilates pupils, releases adrenaline, redirects blood to muscles, suppresses digestion
pathways.bci_relevance: MEDIUM — Spinal cord stimulation can affect sympathetic outflow. Relevant for autonomic side effects of all BCIs near spinal cord.
pathways.disruption_effects: Autonomic storm, hypertension, tachycardia, hyperhidrosis, Horner's syndrome (disruption)
